title: Monopoly
description: Practice what you have learned about the sources of monopolies and how a monopolist makes quantity and pricing decisions in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mammoth Inc. sells computers in a monopoly market.

**Which of the following is true about this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a single firm selling a product with no close substitutes.
The seller can charge any price they want.
There are no, or low, barriers to entry in this market.
The firm earns zero economic profit in the long run.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes a monopoly market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Ethos Unlimited, which is the sole producer of Gleemenex, a drug with no close substitutes.
Pathos, Inc., which produces an antibiotic that is similar to its rival Logos Corp.
Kairos, LLC, which is one of many producers of an identical drug with an expired patent.
Reason, Inc., which is one of the many sellers of a resource with only one buyer.
Rhetorica Unlimited, which is one of many sellers selling a good that is slightly differentiated from other sellers.

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of these are possible reasons for a monopoly to exist EXCEPT which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best describes the relationship between the demand curve (D) and the marginal revenue curve (MR) for a monopoly firm that has a downward sloping linear demand curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: D crosses MR when D is unit elastic
D is greater than MR for some quantities, less than MR for others

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume a monopoly firm has a downward sloping linear demand curve and cannot [[☃ definition 1]]

**What happens to the firm’s marginal revenue as it sells additional units?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: The ability to charge different customers different prices for the same good.

assessment_items.question_content:
The price and total revenue for the monopoly firm Mammoth, Inc. based on the quantity it sells is shown in this table:

Q | Price | Total revenue
-|-|-
0 | $\$20$ | $\$0$
1 | $\$18$ | $\$18$
2 | $\$16$ | $\$32$
3 | $\$14$ | $\$42$
4 | $\$12$ | $\$48$
5 | $\$10$ | $\$50$
6 | $\$8$ | $\$48$

**What is the marginal revenue (MR) for the third unit and the fourth unit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is the profit-maximizing combination of output and price for the single price monopoly shown here?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. Five curves and lines are displayed. The downward-sloping line labeled D represents demand, and another downward-sloping line labeled M R represents marginal revenue. The upward-sloping curve labeled M C starts low, increases steeply as it moves to the right, and intersects the M R line at quantity Q 1 and price P 1. The U-shaped curve labeled A T C starts above the D-line, decreases to a minimum point near quantity Q 2, and then increases to the top right. The U-shaped curve labeled A V C starts below the A T C curve, decreases slightly, and then increases, remaining below the A T C curve. Dashed lines extend horizontally from prices P 1, P 2, P 3, and P 4 to the Y-axis, and vertically from quantities Q 1 and Q 2 to the X-axis, forming a grid to represent equilibrium and cost relationships.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What price will a single price monopolist charge based on this graph?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. The downward-sloping line labeled D represents the demand curve, starting from a high cost on the y-axis and decreasing as quantity increases. Below it, another downward-sloping line labeled M R represents the marginal revenue curve, which declines at a steeper rate than the demand curve. A U-shaped curve labeled A T C represents the average total cost curve, which initially decreases, reaches a minimum point, and then rises as quantity increases. Below it, another U-shaped curve labeled A V C represents the average variable cost curve, following a similar trend but lying entirely below the A T C curve. A curve labeled M C represents the marginal cost curve, which initially decreases before rising sharply. It intersects both the A V C and A T C curves at their lowest points. Horizontal dashed lines extend from five price levels labeled P A, P B, P C, P D, and P E on the y-axis to the demand curve. Vertical dashed lines extend from three quantity levels labeled Q X, Q Y, and Q Z on the x-axis. Curve M C intersects curve A T C at (Q subscript Y, P subscript B). Curve M R intersects curve M C at (Q subscript X, P subscript E).

assessment_items.question_content:
**What price and quantity will this single price monopolist charge?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. Five curves and lines are displayed. The downward-sloping line labeled D represents demand. Another downward-sloping line labeled M R represents marginal revenue. The upward-sloping curve labeled M C starts below all other curves, increases steeply as it moves to the right, and intersects both the A T C curve and the M R line at quantity Q Y. The U-shaped curve labeled A T C starts above the D-line, decreases to a minimum point near quantity Q Y, and then increases to the top right. The U-shaped curve labeled A V C starts below the A T C curve, decreases slightly, and then increases, remaining below the A T C curve. Dashed horizontal lines extend from prices P F, P G, P H, and P I to the Y-axis, while dashed vertical lines extend from quantities Q X and Q Y to the X-axis. The equilibrium occurs at quantity Q Y, where the M C curve intersects the M R line.

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume a monopolist and a perfectly competitive firm face the same market demand and cost curves.

**How does the output and price in a monopoly compare to the output and price in perfect competition?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Monopoly produces a lower quantity and charges a higher price than in perfect competition.
Monopoly produces the same quantity and charges a higher price than in perfect competition.
Monopoly produces a higher quantity and charges a higher price than in perfect competition.
Monopoly produces a lower quantity and charges a lower price than in perfect competition
Monopoly produces a lower quantity and charges the same price as perfect competition.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are the socially optimal price and quantity for the graph shown here?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. Five curves and lines are displayed. The downward-sloping line labeled D represents demand. Another downward-sloping line labeled M R represents marginal revenue. The upward-sloping curve labeled M C starts low, below all other curves, increases steeply as it moves to the right, and intersects the A T C curve and the M R line at quantity Q 1. The U-shaped curve labeled A T C starts above the D-line, decreases to a minimum point, and then increases to the top right. The U-shaped curve labeled A V C starts below the A T C curve, decreases slightly, and then increases, remaining below the A T C curve. Dashed horizontal lines extend from price levels P 1, P 2, P 3, and P 4 to the Y-axis. Dashed vertical lines extend from quantities Q 1 and Q 2 to the X-axis. The equilibrium occurs at quantity Q 1 and price P 1, where the M C curve intersects the M R line.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose that a perfectly competitive firm and a monopoly firm have the same market demand and cost curves, and neither firm wants to shut down given their current demand.

**If the perfectly competitive firm produced the quantity where demand is unit elastic, which of the following is also true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The monopolist produces a quantity on the elastic portion of the demand curve.
The monopolist also produces the unit elastic quantity.
The monopolist produces the quantity that is perfectly elastic.
The monopolist produces a quantity on the inelastic portion of the demand curve.
The monopolist produces the quantity whose marginal revenue equals zero.
